items.title: Your 5 biggest questions about ‘Trump accounts,’ answered
items.description: Here’s who is eligible for $1,000 in free money — and how to sign up.

items.title: Nine microcap stocks of companies expected to grow sales by triple digits through 2028
items.description: The Russell Microcap Index has more than doubled the performance of the S&P 500 this year.

items.title: Your data built the AI boom — but Big Tech is pocketing 100% of the equity
items.description: Your share of the AI wealth is a right — not a handout. Here is how we claw back our money.

items.title: Strategy is losing a lot of money on bitcoin. Here’s why it’s selling anyway.
items.description: Strategy sold more than 3,000 bitcoins to raise cash to pay dividends on its preferred stock — something Executive Chair Michael Saylor had previously said it didn’t need to do.

items.title: You want a portfolio that matches your morals. But that could sink your retirement plans.
items.description: More investors want their money to reflect their values. It feels righteous — but the reality is a lot messier.

items.title: Stocks rally when Congress goes on summer break. Here’s why.
items.description: Stock prices face greater volatility when lawmakers are active, driven entirely by the regulatory uncertainty they create.

items.title: Your investing expectations are more than double the reality. Here is the tough truth.
items.description: Long-term real returns above 10% annualized are exceedingly rare.

items.title: Software stocks have rarely seen such divergent performances. Here’s how to pick winners.
items.description: An Evercore analyst notes that infrastructure software stocks have vastly outperformed application names. But there’s still hope for companies like Salesforce.

items.title: Xbox’s CEO says the business is ‘not healthy’ as it prepares for 3,200 layoffs
items.description: With shares down 20% this year, Microsoft joins peers like Meta and Oracle in using layoffs to offset rising costs of the AI race

items.title: ‘I claimed Social Security at 62’: At 76, I’m working at Walmart. Why do I still owe payroll taxes?
items.description: “It seems like half of the workforce at our local Walmart is over 65.”
